minutes_briefs.title: Occupational therapists can now perform wound care and suctioning — new BC rules
minutes_briefs.summary: The BC government has changed the rules for occupational therapists. Under the new Health and Care Professionals Regulation, occupational therapists can now perform certain restricted activities (medical tasks that are normally only done by doctors or nurses) without needing a separate order each time. Specifically, they can now do wound care (cutting or cleaning tissue below the skin) and airway suctioning (clearing mucus from a patient's breathing tube or nose) if they have special certification. These changes took effect on March 27, 2026.
minutes_briefs.action_required: If you employ occupational therapists, update your clinic's policies and training records to reflect these new allowed activities.

key_dates.label: BC occupational therapist scope expansion takes effect

minutes_briefs.source_body:
Regulation: B.C. Reg. 44/2026
Made under: Health Professions And Occupations Act
Published in: No. 6 – B.C. Regs. 43 to 56, April 7, 2026
Deposit date: March 27, 2026

B.C. Reg. 44/2026, deposited March 27, 2026, under theHEALTH PROFESSIONS AND OCCUPATIONS ACT[section 27]. Ministerial Order M147/2026, dated March 27, 2026. I, Josie Osborne, Minister of Health, order that (a)	the Health and Care Professionals Regulation, B.C. Reg. 131/2025, is amended as set out in the attached Schedule 1, and (b)	the Pharmacists Regulation, B.C. Reg. 135/2025, is amended as set out in the attached Schedule 2. — J. OSBORNE,Minister of Health. 1	Part 5 of the Health and Care Professionals Regulation, B.C. Reg. 131/2025, is amended (a)	by adding the following heading before section 26: Division 1 – General Practice Matters, and (b)	by adding the following Division after section 27: Division 2 – Limited Restricted Activities List of limited restricted activities 27.01An occupational therapist may perform the restricted activities described in the following items of the Restricted Activities Table, but only as provided for in this Division: (a)	item 2[procedures on tissue below dermis]; (c)	items 19, 20 and 24[insertions]. 27.02An occupational therapist may administer oxygen by inhalation. Restricted activities if certified 27.03(1)	A certified occupational therapist may perform a procedure on tissue below the dermis for the purpose of wound care. (2)	A certified occupational therapist may, for the purpose of airway suctioning, insert an instrument, device, hand or finger (a)	beyond the point in the nasal passages where they normally narrow, (c)	into an artificial opening into the body.
